user_story: As a proof author, I can ask for suggested checks without accidentally treating the suggestion as proof.
expected_behavior: Output is a draft profile only; it contains no passed, product_regression, or proof_insufficient verdict.
evidence_required: Generated profile; absence of proof verdict; saved stdout or draft path
finding: Authoring boundary held: suggestions stayed drafts.
next_action: Promote after one more route-specific use against a real product change.

user_story: As a framework maintainer, I can validate core trust-boundary behavior without hosted infrastructure.
expected_behavior: Local core regression pack passes route, query/hash, thrown-error, missing-selector, and no-diff cases.
evidence_required: regression-pack-result.json; required cases present; forbidden terminal markers absent
finding: Core contracts are covered by a repeatable local suite.
next_action: Keep recurring and run before hosted batches.

user_story: As a maintainer, I can run a hosted profile against a live route and receive evidence-backed artifacts.
expected_behavior: Hosted run-profile returns passed and writes profile-result, summary, proof JSON, console, DOM summary, and screenshots.
evidence_required: profile-result.json; proof.json; screenshot; riddle.job_id
finding: Hosted happy path passed with 5/5 checks and artifact links.
next_action: Keep recurring; reuse as known-good packet for reporting and recovery checks.

user_story: As a maintainer, I can run a deliberate missing-selector profile and see product_regression instead of a false pass.
expected_behavior: Negative control returns product_regression with failed-check evidence and artifacts.
evidence_required: profile-result.json; failed check; summary.md; console.json
finding: Negative control stayed red: 1 passed / 1 failed.
next_action: Keep recurring next to hosted happy path.

user_story: As a hosted runner user, I can submit work while workers are scaled to zero without the client declaring the job stale too early.
expected_behavior: Client waits through accepted worker cold-start latency and reports worker-wake progress.
evidence_required: progress output; profile-result.json; queue/pre-submission timing
finding: Cold start waited about 165 seconds and completed without duplicate retry.
next_action: Run occasionally after infra changes; do not wait an hour for every batch.

user_story: As a reviewer, I can get a PR comment whose status, checks, screenshot, and links reflect the saved proof packet.
expected_behavior: PR comment reads profile-result.json, uses profile check statuses, and links hosted artifacts.
evidence_required: pr-comment-body.md; profile-result.json; screenshot/artifact links
finding: Found real bug: profile packets were rejected or miscounted as 4 passed / 12 failed.
next_action: Keep recurring; replay against happy-path and negative-control packets.

user_story: As a profile promoter, I can derive body assertions from a real artifact before making a public claim.
expected_behavior: Body assertion command emits present fragments and exits nonzero for missing required fragments.
evidence_required: body assertion output; nonzero missing-required case
finding: Positive and missing-required cases behaved as a real gate.
next_action: Use when promoting public proof/profile claims.

user_story: As a publisher, I can preflight public artifact URLs before spending hosted browser time.
expected_behavior: Preflight checks status, content type, bytes, required body fragments, forbidden body text, and JSON assertions.
evidence_required: preflight JSON; failed missing-fragment negative control
finding: Live CDN artifact passed positive checks; missing fragment failed clearly.
next_action: Keep as the cheap gate before broad hosted runs.

user_story: As a user whose local process was interrupted, I can recover a hosted job and still get the proof artifacts and summary.
expected_behavior: Recover preserves the original job id and writes profile-result, summary, proof JSON, console, DOM summary, and screenshot references.
evidence_required: recovered profile-result.json; summary.md; proof.json; preserved job id
finding: Recovery reconstructed the hosted happy-path packet from the old job id.
next_action: Keep as recurring for interrupted-run confidence.

user_story: As a new user, I can invoke the published package binary from a clean temporary directory.
expected_behavior: npm exec resolves riddle-proof-loop and prints CLI help when npm is on PATH.
evidence_required: npm exec output; package version
finding: Earlier command-not-found was local PATH, not a package bin defect.
next_action: Repeat after package metadata or bin changes.

user_story: As a user avoiding hosted infra, I can run the local Playwright runner and receive the same profile-result contract shape.
expected_behavior: riddle-proof-playwright writes profile-result.json, proof.json, console.json, dom-summary.json, summary.md, and artifact-manifest.json.
evidence_required: profile-result.json; artifact-manifest.json; screenshot or DOM evidence
finding: Live local Playwright smoke passed against riddledc.com/proof with 5/5 checks.
next_action: Keep as local-vs-hosted parity smoke; rerun after runner package changes.

user_story: As a maintainer running viewport matrices, I can aggregate child runs without losing the failing viewport.
expected_behavior: Aggregate returns the worst child verdict, lists child outputs, and separates summed poll time from max child latency.
evidence_required: child profile-result files; aggregate profile-result.json; summary.md
finding: Aggregate preserved the phone child product_regression via split_viewport_children.
next_action: Keep as a recurring negative-control aggregate using one passing child and one failing child.

user_story: As a framework maintainer, I can prove the status/result contract that runtime tests depend on.
expected_behavior: Lean kernel builds and runtime conformance tests mirror the formal contract cases.
evidence_required: lake build; formal-conformance test log
finding: Formal layer stayed in contract-hardening path, not runtime evidence collection.
next_action: Keep recurring when status semantics change.

user_story: As a hosted proof viewer, I see a passing packet rendered from the shared public-state contract.
expected_behavior: Hosted proof view helper reports passed proof state only from passed/ready evidence and preserves handoff gating.
evidence_required: summarizeRiddleProofHostedProofViewSurface passed case; public-state claims; handoff claims
finding: Shared hosted-view helper uses the same public-state contract as PR comments.
next_action: Use this helper from any real hosted proof packet renderer.

user_story: As a hosted proof viewer, I see product_regression as failed evidence, not as a generic successful packet.
expected_behavior: product_regression maps to proof_failed, keeps result_label product_regression, and prohibits proof_passed/ready_to_ship/merge claims.
evidence_required: product_regression packet; prohibited claims; suppressed merge recommendation
finding: Found and fixed the semantic gap where profile verdicts were not explicit public-state blockers.
next_action: Keep this as the negative-control renderer contract before UI smoke.

user_story: As a hosted proof viewer, I see missing or incomplete evidence as proof_insufficient with an explicit disclosure.
expected_behavior: proof_insufficient maps to proof_blocked, keeps result_label proof_insufficient, and requires proof_insufficient disclosure.
evidence_required: proof_insufficient packet; disclosure; prohibited proof_passed claim
finding: Incomplete evidence now stays blocked in public consumer surfaces.
next_action: Keep paired with product_regression in renderer smoke tests.

user_story: As a hosted proof viewer, the actual product renderer imports the shared contract instead of rendering raw proof fields.
expected_behavior: Hosted proof packet UI uses @riddledc/riddle-proof/public-state or an equivalent shared wrapper for status, handoff, and disclosures.
evidence_required: renderer import; screenshot; passed and negative-control packets
finding: Read-only site scan found proof/example pages, but no live hosted proof packet renderer importing public-state yet.
next_action: Wire any hosted proof packet renderer to summarizeRiddleProofHostedProofViewSurface and run passed plus negative-control UI smoke.

user_story: As an agent consuming Riddle Proof output, I see proof_insufficient as incomplete evidence, not as success.
expected_behavior: Agent summary helper maps proof_insufficient to proof_blocked and prohibits proof_passed.
evidence_required: summarizeRiddleProofAgentSummarySurface proof_insufficient case; disclosure; prohibited claim
finding: Agent-facing proof_insufficient summaries now share the public-state blocker contract.
next_action: Keep as a fixture for agent-output golden summaries.

user_story: As an agent consuming Riddle Proof output, I see environment_blocked as a blocker outside product correctness.
expected_behavior: Agent summary helper maps environment_blocked to proof_blocked, suppresses merge recommendations, and requires environment disclosure.
evidence_required: environment_blocked packet; suppressed merge recommendation; disclosure
finding: Environment blockers stay visible and cannot become proof_passed in agent summaries.
next_action: Use in hosted cold-start/network-blocked summary tests.

user_story: As an agent consuming Riddle Proof output, I see needs_human_review as a held proof state, not a pass.
expected_behavior: Agent summary helper maps needs_human_review to proof_blocked and requires human-review disclosure.
evidence_required: needs_human_review packet; disclosure; prohibited proof_passed claim
finding: Human-review verdicts now stay blocked in public consumer surfaces.
next_action: Keep this for evidence packets with subjective or unsupported checks.

user_story: As an agent consuming real Riddle Proof output, the emitted summary uses the shared contract rather than raw fields.
expected_behavior: Agent-facing summaries import/use the public-state consumer helper for status, handoff, and prohibited claims.
evidence_required: summary text; contract import or documented gap; negative-control packet
finding: Package helper is now contract-tested; product wiring still needs an executable consumer check outside PR comments.
next_action: Find the actual agent summary renderer/emitter and wire or verify summarizeRiddleProofAgentSummarySurface usage.

user_story: As a consumer of Riddle Proof summaries, I can validate status semantics on neutral packets rather than Riddle docs packets.
expected_behavior: Neutral public-state fixtures cover passed handoff, product_regression, proof_insufficient, environment_blocked, and checkpoint audit claims.
evidence_required: fixture JSON; hosted proof view helper; agent summary helper
finding: Neutral packet fixtures keep public-state semantics separate from Riddle docs dogfood.
next_action: Keep this as the fast semantic canary before local, hosted, docs, and real-app sweeps.

user_story: As a maintainer, I can run a non-Riddle static fixture page locally and get a normal evidence packet.
expected_behavior: Local Playwright pass profile returns passed with proof JSON, console, DOM summary, artifact manifest, and screenshots.
evidence_required: profile-result.json; artifact-manifest.json; screenshot; proof.json
finding: Neutral static fixture produced a passed local evidence packet across phone and desktop.
next_action: Keep as the clean non-docs local canary before real-app sweeps.

user_story: As a maintainer, I can run a non-Riddle negative-control profile and get product_regression instead of a false pass.
expected_behavior: Local Playwright negative-control profile fails only the deliberate missing selector and preserves review artifacts.
evidence_required: profile-result.json; failed check; screenshot; proof.json
finding: Neutral negative control stayed red on [data-rp-fixture="missing-required-control"] while preserving artifacts.
next_action: Keep paired with the pass fixture before broader UX sweeps.

user_story: As a maintainer, I can run a non-Riddle static fixture preview on hosted Riddle and get a normal evidence packet.
expected_behavior: Hosted pass profile returns passed with hosted job id, proof JSON, console, DOM summary, and screenshots.
evidence_required: profile-result.json; riddle.job_id; screenshot URLs; proof.json
finding: Hosted neutral pass produced job_2ab0f3f3 after fixture artifact requirements were calibrated.
next_action: Keep as the clean hosted canary before docs dogfood and real-app sweeps.

user_story: As a maintainer, I can run a non-Riddle hosted negative-control profile and get product_regression instead of a false pass.
expected_behavior: Hosted negative-control profile reaches the static preview and fails only the deliberate missing selector.
evidence_required: profile-result.json; failed check; screenshot URLs; proof.json
finding: Hosted neutral negative control produced job_b308bc57 with product_regression and preserved artifact URLs.
next_action: Keep paired with the hosted pass fixture before broader hosted UX sweeps.

user_story: As a package maintainer, I can merge a fix, get a version PR, and publish the package without manual status fakery.
expected_behavior: Changeset release PR validates, merges, and trusted npm publish updates latest.
evidence_required: release PR; Release workflow; npm view latest
finding: Release path worked for the reporting fix and published with provenance.
next_action: Keep GitHub Action noise low and use this as release smoke.
